refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving or material-handling tasks such that the cumulative productive output (measured in m³/h or ton/h) meets or exceeds the customer-defined productivity target over a representative evaluation period, accounting for task cycle time, material density, and operational availability.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator control fidelity, machine availability, and measurement/reporting. Refinement separates the functional intent (enable productive work) from the measurement and acceptance criteria. The refined FR acknowledges that productivity is a system-level outcome dependent on vehicle performance, teleoperation quality (if applicable), and operational continuity.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative field trials over the agreed evaluation period (e.g., 40 operating hours). Measure cumulative material moved (m³ or ton) and divide by elapsed productive time (h). Compare against customer-defined target. Document task types, cycle times, idle periods, and any downtime. Validate measurement methodology with Site Manager and Maintenance stakeholder.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving/material-handling tasks with cycle time and precision sufficient to achieve target throughput.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system (if deployed) shall provide operator control authority and feedback fidelity sufficient to maintain task execution rate equivalent to on-site operation.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall maintain operational availability (uptime) such that unplanned downtime does not reduce productivity below target.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) to the Site Manager for monitoring and acceptance validation.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate a high-capacity vehicle with optimized hydraulic actuation, bucket geometry, and cycle-time tuning to maximize material throughput per unit time.

DP-B: Combine vehicle task execution with real-time teleoperation capability (low-latency control and high-fidelity video/sensor feedback) to enable remote operators to maintain on-site productivity rates.
DP-C: Implement predictive maintenance and redundant subsystems (engine, hydraulics, electrical) to minimize unplanned downtime and sustain high operational availability.

DP-D: Deploy an integrated telemetry and analytics platform that continuously measures task cycle time, material volume, and idle periods, aggregating into real-time and historical productivity dashboards accessible to Site Manager.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-Integrated: Combine DP-A (vehicle task execution optimization), DP-B (teleoperation fidelity), DP-C (availability assurance), and DP-D (productivity measurement and reporting) into a cohesive system design. Productivity is achieved through the synergy of capable task execution, operator control (on-site or remote), system reliability, and transparent measurement.

potential_sources: DP-A (vehicle performance) and DP-B (teleoperation fidelity) are coupled: remote operator control quality directly affects achievable cycle time; poor teleoperation feedback may degrade task execution rate below on-site baseline.
DP-C (availability) and DP-A (task execution) are coupled: vehicle downtime directly reduces cumulative productivity; maintenance scheduling must not conflict with evaluation period.

DP-D (measurement) depends on all other DPs: accurate productivity metrics require reliable data from vehicle sensors, teleoperation logs, and availability records; sensor failures or data loss invalidate measurement.
DP-B and DP-D are coupled: teleoperation system must provide low-latency telemetry to measurement platform; network latency or packet loss affects both control fidelity and data integrity.
coupling_check.notes: The refined FR is inherently multi-dimensional (task execution, control, availability, measurement). A single DP cannot satisfy it without decomposition. The selected DP is a composite that acknowledges these couplings but defers detailed decoupling to the next decomposition level. Recommend zigzag down to refine each sub-DP and identify decoupling strategies (e.g., redundant telemetry paths, predictive maintenance scheduling outside evaluation window).

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Customer-defined productivity target is realistic and achievable with available vehicle technology and operator skill level.
Assumption: Evaluation period is representative of typical worksite conditions (soil type, weather, task mix).
Assumption: Teleoperation latency can be maintained below operator perception threshold (~200 ms round-trip) to avoid degradation of task execution rate.

Risk: If vehicle cycle time is inherently slower than on-site baseline due to remote control lag, productivity target may be unachievable; mitigation requires autonomous task assistance or predictive control.
Risk: Unplanned downtime during evaluation period (e.g., hydraulic failure, electrical fault) could invalidate acceptance; mitigation requires redundancy or maintenance scheduling discipline.
Risk: Measurement system failure (sensor malfunction, data loss) could prevent credible productivity validation; mitigation requires sensor redundancy and data integrity checks.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Decompose the integrated DP into four distinct sub-DPs (one per sub-FR) and explicitly model the coupling dependencies. For each sub-DP, identify decoupling strategies (e.g., teleoperation with predictive control to reduce latency sensitivity, redundant telemetry paths, maintenance scheduling outside evaluation window). Establish acceptance criteria for each sub-DP (e.g., teleoperation latency ≤ 200 ms, vehicle availability ≥ 95%, measurement accuracy ±5%).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution is the top-level system boundary encompassing all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and their integration. At this decomposition level, the IM assignment reflects that CR-NGHE-001 is a system-level KPI that cannot be localized to a single module; instead, it is realized through the coordinated operation of all modules. The NGHE Solution IM serves as the integration point where vehicle task execution, teleoperation control, safety constraints, and productivity measurement converge.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs real-time and historical productivity metrics (m³/h, ton/h, cycle time, idle time, material volume) to external systems (Site Manager dashboard, customer reporting portal). Telemetry includes vehicle state (position, bucket load, engine RPM), operator input (remote or on-site), and system health (availability, fault codes).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives task directives from Site Manager (e.g., 'move material from pile A to pile B', 'grade area X to elevation Y'). Tasking may include productivity targets, time windows, and safety constraints. Tasking is translated into vehicle control commands and operator guidance.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs safety alerts (e.g., proximity warnings, stability alerts, operator fatigue warnings) to Safety Manager and on-site personnel. SafetyAlert is triggered by Safety Services module and propagated through NGHE Solution to external stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity for teleoperation control (if deployed), telemetry transmission, and safety alert distribution. Network Link is critical for remote operation and real-time productivity monitoring. Latency and reliability of Network Link directly affect achievable productivity.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary. CR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI) is inherently a system-level requirement that depends on the coordinated operation of all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). Containment is high because the IM boundary encompasses all necessary subsystems. Diffusion is minimal because no external system is required to realize productivity; however, external measurement and reporting (Telemetry interface) is necessary for acceptance validation.

Vehicle module realizes task execution (DP-A) but does not independently achieve productivity KPI. Vehicle performance (cycle time, throughput) is necessary but not sufficient; operator control quality (Teleoperation), system availability (maintenance), and measurement (Telemetry) are also required. Assigning CR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse responsibility for teleoperation fidelity, availability, and measurement to external modules, violating cohesion.

Teleoperation module realizes operator control (DP-B) but does not independently achieve productivity KPI. Remote control fidelity is necessary but not sufficient; vehicle capability (Vehicle), system availability, and measurement are also required. Assigning CR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would diffuse responsibility for vehicle performance and availability to external modules.

Safety Services module is orthogonal to productivity KPI. While safety constraints may limit achievable productivity (e.g., speed restrictions in hazardous areas), Safety Services does not directly realize productivity. Assigning CR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services would create high diffusion and violate separation of concerns.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: CR-NGHE-001 is a system-level KPI that emerges from the coordinated operation of Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (operator control), and Safety Services (safety constraints). No single module can independently achieve productivity; instead, the NGHE Solution IM boundary serves as the integration point where all subsystems contribute. Ownership of CR-NGHE-001 should reside with the System Engineer or Integration Lead responsible for end-to-end system performance and acceptance validation.
containment_risks: Risk: If Vehicle module is optimized for throughput without coordination with Teleoperation, remote operator may not be able to maintain on-site productivity rates due to control lag or feedback latency. Mitigation: Establish interface contracts between Vehicle and Teleoperation specifying maximum acceptable latency and control authority.

Risk: If Teleoperation module is designed without awareness of Vehicle capabilities, remote control commands may exceed vehicle actuation limits, causing jerky motion and reduced operator effectiveness. Mitigation: Teleoperation must model Vehicle dynamics and provide predictive control or haptic feedback.

Risk: If Safety Services module imposes speed or acceleration limits without coordination with productivity targets, achievable productivity may fall below target. Mitigation: Safety Services must be designed with awareness of productivity constraints; safety limits should be optimized to maximize safe productivity.

Risk: If Telemetry measurement is not integrated with Vehicle and Teleoperation, productivity metrics may be inaccurate or delayed, preventing real-time monitoring and acceptance validation. Mitigation: Telemetry must be fed by Vehicle sensors and Teleoperation logs with synchronized timestamps and validated data integrity.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Maintain NGHE Solution as the IM boundary for CR-NGHE-001. However, decompose the requirement into four sub-FRs (one per sub-DP) and assign each sub-FR to the appropriate module: FR-NGHE-001.1 (vehicle task execution) → Vehicle; FR-NGHE-001.2 (teleoperation fidelity) → Teleoperation; FR-NGHE-001.3 (operational availability) → Vehicle + Maintenance subsystem; FR-NGHE-001.4 (productivity measurement) → NGHE Solution (integration). Establish interface contracts between modules specifying latency, control authority, data formats, and measurement accuracy. Define acceptance criteria for each sub-FR and aggregate into system-level productivity KPI.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FR is multi-dimensional (task execution, operator control, availability, measurement) and the selected DP is a composite that acknowledges multiple couplings but does not fully decouple them. The FR-DP mapping is incomplete at this level because the DP does not specify how to decouple the interdependencies between vehicle performance, teleoperation fidelity, availability, and measurement. Recommendation: Decompose the FR into four atomic sub-FRs and select a distinct DP for each, with explicit decoupling strategies (e.g., teleoperation with predictive control, redundant telemetry paths, maintenance scheduling). Revise the current level before proceeding to IM assignment.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: If Vehicle module is assigned task execution (DP-A) without explicit interface contracts with Teleoperation, remote operator control quality may degrade task execution rate. Mitigation: Define latency and control authority contracts between Vehicle and Teleoperation.

Diffusion Risk 2: If Teleoperation module is assigned operator control (DP-B) without awareness of Vehicle dynamics, remote control commands may exceed vehicle limits. Mitigation: Teleoperation must model Vehicle dynamics and provide predictive control.

Diffusion Risk 3: If availability assurance (DP-C) is split between Vehicle (maintenance) and external maintenance contractor, responsibility for downtime may be unclear. Mitigation: Establish clear ownership and SLA for maintenance scheduling and response time.

Diffusion Risk 4: If Telemetry measurement (DP-D) is implemented without integration with Vehicle sensors and Teleoperation logs, data accuracy and timeliness may be compromised. Mitigation: Telemetry must be fed by Vehicle and Teleoperation with synchronized timestamps and validated data integrity.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution IM boundary is appropriate for CR-NGHE-001 because it encompasses all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) required to achieve productivity. However, the composite DP must be decomposed into sub-DPs assigned to specific modules with explicit interface contracts to manage diffusion risks. The current DP-IM mapping is acceptable as a system-level integration point but requires detailed sub-level decomposition to ensure cohesion and minimize diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-001 is feasible through field trials measuring cumulative material moved (m³ or ton) over the evaluation period and comparing against customer-defined target. Verification requires: (1) reliable Telemetry interface to capture vehicle state and task execution data, (2) synchronized logging of operator input (on-site or remote) and vehicle response, (3) accurate measurement of material volume and density, (4) documentation of downtime and availability. Verification is dependent on the quality of the Telemetry interface and the accuracy of the measurement system (DP-D). Recommend establishing measurement methodology and acceptance criteria with Site Manager and Maintenance stakeholder before evaluation period.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation of CR-NGHE-001 to NGHE Solution is clear at the system level, but allocation to sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) requires decomposition. The refined FR includes four sub-FRs that can be allocated to specific modules: FR-NGHE-001.1 → Vehicle, FR-NGHE-001.2 → Teleoperation, FR-NGHE-001.3 → Vehicle + Maintenance, FR-NGHE-001.4 → NGHE Solution (integration). Recommend establishing allocation matrix and interface contracts before detailed design.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1 (FR-DP Gate): Decompose the refined FR into four atomic sub-FRs and select a distinct DP for each. Explicitly model coupling dependencies and identify decoupling strategies (e.g., teleoperation with predictive control, redundant telemetry paths, maintenance scheduling outside evaluation window). Establish acceptance criteria for each sub-DP.

Revision 2 (DP-IM Gate): For each sub-DP, assign to the appropriate module (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, or NGHE Solution integration). Establish interface contracts specifying latency, control authority, data formats, and measurement accuracy. Define ownership and responsibility for each sub-DP.

Revision 3 (FR-IM Gate): Establish measurement methodology and acceptance criteria for CR-NGHE-001 in coordination with Site Manager and Maintenance stakeholder. Define Telemetry interface requirements (data types, sampling rate, accuracy) to support productivity measurement. Validate that Telemetry interface can reliably capture necessary data for acceptance validation.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level system KPI that is not atomic and cannot be fully satisfied by a single DP or module at this level. The refined FR identifies four distinct sub-FRs (vehicle task execution, teleoperation fidelity, operational availability, productivity measurement) that require separate DPs and module assignments. The FR-DP coupling check reveals multiple interdependencies (vehicle performance ↔ teleoperation fidelity, availability ↔ task execution, measurement ↔ all other DPs) that must be explicitly managed through interface contracts and decoupling strategies. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to: (1) decouple the four sub-FRs into independent design problems, (2) assign each sub-FR to the appropriate module with clear ownership, (3) establish interface contracts and acceptance criteria, (4) identify and mitigate diffusion risks. The current level provides the system-level context and integration strategy; the next level will provide detailed module-level design and verification plans.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving/material-handling tasks with cycle time and precision sufficient to achieve target throughput (m³/h or ton/h). [Assigned to: Vehicle module]

FR-NGHE-001.2: The teleoperation system shall provide operator control authority and feedback fidelity sufficient to maintain task execution rate equivalent to on-site operation (latency ≤ 200 ms, control authority ≥ 95%). [Assigned to: Teleoperation module]

FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall maintain operational availability (uptime ≥ 95%) such that unplanned downtime does not reduce productivity below target. [Assigned to: Vehicle module + Maintenance subsystem]

FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h) with accuracy ±5% to the Site Manager for monitoring and acceptance validation. [Assigned to: NGHE Solution integration + Telemetry interface]

assumptions: Assumption A1: Customer-defined productivity target is realistic and achievable with available vehicle technology and operator skill level. Evidence needed: Baseline productivity data from similar equipment and worksite conditions.

Assumption A2: Evaluation period is representative of typical worksite conditions (soil type, weather, task mix). Evidence needed: Worksite characterization and task mix documentation from Site Manager.

Assumption A3: Teleoperation latency can be maintained below operator perception threshold (~200 ms round-trip) to avoid degradation of task execution rate. Evidence needed: Teleoperation system latency measurements and operator feedback during trials.
Assumption A4: Vehicle cycle time is not inherently slower than on-site baseline due to remote control lag. Evidence needed: Comparative trials of on-site vs. remote operation with same operator.
Assumption A5: Measurement system can reliably capture productivity metrics with accuracy ±5%. Evidence needed: Sensor calibration data, data integrity validation, and measurement methodology review.
open_risks: Risk R1 (High): If vehicle cycle time is inherently slower than on-site baseline due to remote control lag, productivity target may be unachievable. Mitigation: Implement autonomous task assistance or predictive control in Teleoperation module. Contingency: Revise productivity target downward based on teleoperation performance data.

Risk R2 (High): Unplanned downtime during evaluation period (e.g., hydraulic failure, electrical fault) could invalidate acceptance. Mitigation: Implement redundancy in critical subsystems (engine, hydraulics, electrical) and schedule maintenance outside evaluation window. Contingency: Extend evaluation period or conduct multiple trials.

Risk R3 (Medium): Measurement system failure (sensor malfunction, data loss) could prevent credible productivity validation. Mitigation: Implement sensor redundancy and data integrity checks. Contingency: Use manual measurement as backup.

Risk R4 (Medium): Safety constraints (speed limits, proximity warnings) may reduce achievable productivity below target. Mitigation: Optimize safety limits to maximize safe productivity. Contingency: Coordinate with Safety Manager to adjust safety constraints if necessary.

Risk R5 (Medium): Teleoperation network latency may exceed acceptable threshold due to poor connectivity at worksite. Mitigation: Conduct network assessment and implement redundant communication paths. Contingency: Deploy local edge computing or autonomous control to reduce latency sensitivity.
evidence_needed: Evidence E1: Baseline productivity data from similar equipment and worksite conditions (to validate assumption A1).
Evidence E2: Worksite characterization and task mix documentation (to validate assumption A2).
Evidence E3: Teleoperation system latency measurements and operator feedback during trials (to validate assumption A3).
Evidence E4: Comparative trials of on-site vs. remote operation with same operator (to validate assumption A4).
Evidence E5: Sensor calibration data, data integrity validation, and measurement methodology review (to validate assumption A5).
Evidence E6: Vehicle cycle time data from representative tasks (to assess risk R1).
Evidence E7: Reliability data for critical subsystems (engine, hydraulics, electrical) and maintenance history (to assess risk R2).
Evidence E8: Sensor redundancy design and data integrity validation procedures (to assess risk R3).
Evidence E9: Safety constraint analysis and impact on productivity (to assess risk R4).
Evidence E10: Network assessment and latency measurements at worksite (to assess risk R5).
